title: [lichen_l2.c:640] lichen_l2_enable acquires both mutexes with nested locking
description: Function: lichen_l2_enable. Thread safety: The function acquires tx_mutex then rx_mutex (lines 695-696 enable path, 778-779 disable path). The comment at lines 687-692 documents this ordering. However, if any future code acquires rx_mutex then tx_mutex (ABBA pattern), deadlock would occur. The code currently maintains consistent ordering but no static analysis enforces this. Consider adding a comment at mutex definitions or using Zephyr's lock ordering annotations if available. Priority 3 - correctly ordered, documentation improvement.
close_reason: Fixed: added lock order documentation at mutex definitions (~line 217) with canonical LOCK ORDER comment explaining tx_mutex must be acquired before rx_mutex. Updated comments in lichen_l2_enable() to reference the canonical location. Lock order was already consistent across all call sites - this fix documents it properly.
